title: Luscious Indian Pork Tenderloin Skillet
description: Buttery, cheese-pull, weeknight Indian dish featuring pork tenderloin in a skillet.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the olive oil.
Add the sliced pork and sear until browned on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Sprinkle the garam masala, cumin, paprika, turmeric, salt, and pepper over the pork and stir to coat evenly.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Reduce the heat to low and spread the shredded cheddar cheese evenly over the pork and onion mixture.
Cover the skillet with a lid and let the cheese melt for 1-2 minutes until bubbly.
Use a spatula to gently drape the melted cheese over the pork slices, creating a pull-apart effect.
Drizzle a little butter on top of the cheese for extra richness and let it melt.
Cook the pork until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C), about 8-10 more minutes total.
Serve hot with steamed rice or naan and enjoy the buttery, cheese-pull experience.
